# @http-forge/playwright

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40http-forge%2Fplaywright)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@http-forge/playwright)
[![npm downloads](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/%40http-forge%2Fplaywright)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@http-forge/playwright)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/%40http-forge%2Fplaywright)](LICENSE)
[![node](https://img.shields.io/node/v/%40http-forge%2Fplaywright)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@http-forge/playwright)

**The runtime and shared types package for Playwright clients generated by HTTP Forge.**

Use this with `@http-forge/codegen` to run typed API client functions in Playwright tests with consistent environment interpolation and URL building.

## Who It Is For

- Teams generating typed API clients from HTTP Forge workspaces.
- Playwright users who want reusable request runtime utilities.
- QA and automation engineers who need environment-aware API tests.
- CI/CD pipelines running generated clients in Playwright.

## Why @http-forge/playwright

- Keep generated files small by centralizing runtime behavior.
- Use `ForgeEnv` for `{{variable}}` interpolation and path/query URL composition.
- Share common request option/types across generated client files.
- Keep Playwright API test code consistent across projects.

## How It Fits In HTTP Forge Family

| Component | Role |
|---|---|
| HTTP Forge VS Code extension | Build and validate requests/collections in workspace |
| `@http-forge/core` | Shared execution and Postman-compatible runtime behavior |
| `@http-forge/codegen` | Generates typed TypeScript API client functions |
| `@http-forge/playwright` | Runtime + shared types used by generated Playwright clients |
| HTTP Forge CLI | Headless collection/suite runs and reporting for CI/CD |

`@http-forge/playwright` is focused on generated Playwright client runtime. It does not replace extension UI workflows or CLI collection execution.

## 1-Minute Quickstart

```bash
# 1) Install
npm install @http-forge/playwright @playwright/test

# 2) Generate clients (via @http-forge/codegen in your flow)
# 3) Use generated client + ForgeEnv in Playwright tests
```

```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
import { ForgeEnv } from '@http-forge/playwright';
import { loginRequest } from './generated/login-request';

test('login flow', async ({ request }) => {
    const env = ForgeEnv.create({
        baseUrl: 'https://auth.example.com',
        clientId: 'my-client-id'
    });

    const response = await loginRequest({
        request,
        env,
        query: {
            client_id: '{{clientId}}',
            response_type: 'code'
        }
    });

    expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();
});
```

## Core Exports

```ts
import { ForgeEnv, type IForgeEnv } from '@http-forge/playwright';
import { ForgeEnv as ForgeEnvRuntime } from '@http-forge/playwright/forge-env';
import type { HttpHeaders, BaseRequestContext, BaseApiOptions } from '@http-forge/playwright/shared-types';
```
